articles.title: China’s Aiming for the Moon, and NASA Is Looking Over Its Shoulder
articles.summary: The U.S. space agency launched a lunar flyby Wednesday, but Beijing is pursuing its own space program with formidable focus. Here’s what we know about it, in photos and videos.

articles.title: 25 years ago today, a mid-air collision sparked an 11-day U.S.–China standoff
articles.summary: On April 1, 2001, a midair collision between an American aircraft and a Chinese plane sparked an 11-day diplomatic crisis — and the two countries still tell very different stories.

articles.title: Artemis II Completes First Day of Its NASA Lunar Mission
articles.summary: The crew, three Americans and a Canadian, are the first humans to travel to the moon in more than 50 years. They will not land on the surface, but the mission will pave the way for future visits.

articles.title: The Artemis II Toilet Had a Problem
articles.summary: The Universal Waste Management System aboard the Orion capsule is an innovation in deep space toiletry (it seems to be fixed now).

articles.title: Iran War Showcases Strength of South Korean Defense Sector
articles.summary: Missile interceptors made by the South Korean firm LIG Nex1 are said to be performing well, at a small fraction of the cost of U.S. interceptors.

articles.title: Republicans in Congress say they have a deal to end the record-long shutdown at DHS
articles.summary: The plan would fund DHS, except for immigration enforcement, through September. Republicans would then try to fund the whole agency for three years using a tactic that would not need Democratic votes.

articles.title: 5 Takeaways From Trump’s Address on Iran
articles.summary: President Trump did not define a clear path out of the conflict, which he estimated would end within three weeks.

articles.title: Trump Initially Laid Out Five Goals for the Iran War. Here’s Where They Stand.
articles.summary: The United States and Israel have done significant damage to Iran’s military capabilities. But Iran still fires missiles, has nuclear material and coordinates with militias in the region.

articles.title: Appeals court rejects HUD homelessness overhaul saying it would be "disastrous"
articles.summary: The federal housing agency wants to shift money away from permanent housing and toward programs that impose sobriety and other conditions. Advocates warn that would push many back into homelessness.

articles.title: Trump Has Discussed Firing Attorney General Pam Bondi
articles.summary: President Trump has not made a final decision, but he has floated the idea of replacing Ms. Bondi with Lee Zeldin, the E.P.A. administrator.

articles.title: FIFA hikes World Cup prices, again. A finals ticket can now cost as much as $11,000
articles.summary: FIFA has raised prices for over 40 games of the World Cup in its latest sale that kicked off Wednesday, according to an NPR count. The most expensive tickets to the final now cost $10,900.

articles.title: Senate and House Republicans Strike Deal to End DHS Shutdown
articles.summary: A bill to reopen the department, which the House G.O.P. rejected on Friday, could be approved as early as Thursday. It was a sharp turnaround by the lawmakers and President Trump.

articles.title: Who is held accountable if a war crime is committed in Iran?
articles.summary: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with international law expert Gabor Rona about attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Iran conflict and how they may constitute war crimes.

articles.title: The FDA approves a new obesity pill, giving patients another option
articles.summary: The daily pill called Foundayo got a fast track through the Food and Drug Administration. It will compete with the pill form of Wegovy as an alternative to obesity drugs given by injection.

articles.title: A Turkish border town known for its cats - in times of peace
articles.summary: The border town of Van in Turkey is known for being a hub for Iranian travelers, its lavish breakfasts ... and its cats.

articles.title: Is the U.S. Navy ready to clear sea mines in the Persian Gulf?
articles.summary: Despite the danger of sea mines, experts say that mine clearing has received minimal attention and funding from the U.S. Navy — and it's often overshadowed by more high-profile weapons systems.

articles.title: "Lives will be lost": How the U.K.'s aid cuts may affect parts of Africa
articles.summary: In March, the UK announced it would trim its global aid budget and set new priorities in 2027. This has some countries and organizations worried that on top of the US aid cuts, this could be unsurviva

articles.title: This fringe actor finds the spotlight in Israel's most provocative film post-Oct. 7
articles.summary: Israeli fringe actor in the spotlight for anti-war film

articles.title: Trump makes his case for war with Iran, saying the conflict is 'nearing completion'
articles.summary: Trump used a prime-time address to outline his objectives for the war and discuss the military's progress to date. He also repeated an earlier claim that fighting could end in two to three weeks.

articles.title: After Israel's invasion, many in southern Lebanon worry they'll never go home
articles.summary: Israel says it's creating a "security zone" inside Lebanon, where homes will be demolished, and residents won't be allowed to return home until people in northern Israel feel safe.

articles.title: Greetings from London, where a big COVID memorial holds vigil over the halls of power
articles.summary: Britain's 1,640-foot-long National Covid Memorial Wall stretches along the River Thames, with over a quarter million hearts representing the lives lost during the pandemic — across from Parliament.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
